items.title: All Eyes On December Fed Meeting As Traders Await Interest Rate Projections
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: The Federal Reserve is expected to cut interest rates by 0.25% at its December meeting, bringing the federal funds rate to 4.25%-4.50%. Traders are more focused on the Fed's updated economic projections and guidance, which could reshape expectations for 2025 rate cuts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article suggests that buying the S&P 500 index (tracked by the SPDR S&P 500 ETF) around Fed meetings has historically yielded positive returns, indicating a positive sentiment towards the ETF.

items.title: The S&P 500 Recently Hit Another Record High. Should You Wait Until the New Year to Invest in It?
items.author: Stefon Walters
items.description: The S&P 500 has hit a new all-time high, leading some to hesitate about investing. However, the article advises against trying to time the market and instead recommends using dollar-cost averaging to invest in the S&P 500 consistently over time.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article recommends the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF as a low-cost and liquid option to invest in the S&P 500 index.
The article compares the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF to the SPDR S&P 500 Trust ETF, noting that the Vanguard ETF has a lower expense ratio.
